steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: The left side of the blank creates an independent clause with a participial phrase at the end (raising the surrounding air temperature). The right side, with Adding, forms an independent clause where a gerund phrase serves as the subject of the verb helps.

steps.title: Apply the sentence boundary rule
steps.content: Two independent clauses require a period to separate them and prevent a run-on sentence. Choice C correctly places temperature. Adding to create this boundary. The other choices fail because they either use inappropriate punctuation (A's semicolon, B's comma) or omit it entirely (D), leaving independent clauses improperly connected. That matches choice C — C is correct.
